pending.body:
finding_summary: Accepted propose-new-bead work can be silently dropped on transient br/journal errors. `reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success` only logs and continues when `handle_propose_new_bead` fails, so the active bead still closes even if the follow-up bead was never created or synced. Because this path does not persist a retry marker or escalate to needs-operator, an accepted final-review finding can disappear from the backlog after a single failed reconciliation pass.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:247-255
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:979-1025
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2955-3147
remediation_hint: Treat propose-new-bead reconciliation as durable work: either propagate the failure so success reconciliation does not finish cleanly, or persist an explicit retry/operator-needed record before returning.

finding_summary: The defensive duplicate check ignores existing deferred beads, so propose-new-bead can create a second copy of work that is already planned. `list_all()` explicitly searches deferred issues, but `eligible_existing_proposed_bead_match` rejects every status except open/in_progress; if the matching owner bead is deferred, the handler falls through to creation instead of reclassifying to planned-elsewhere.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:173-179
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2768-2770
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2975-3053
remediation_hint: Treat `Deferred` as an eligible existing owner for the defensive re-check, or explicitly reopen/defer-map that bead instead of creating a duplicate.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
